title: verilator improperly warns about mismatched widths
body:
---


Author Name: **Jonathon Donaldson**
Original Redmine Issue: 779 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2014-06-05


---

```module example (
  input         i_clk,
  input  [ 7:0] i_sample_narrow,
  output [13:0] o_sample_wide
);

always @(posedge i_clk)
  o_sample_wide <= $signed(i_sample_narrow);

endmodule
```

For the above code I get the following warning:
```$ verilator --lint-only example.v
%Warning-WIDTH: example.v:8: Operator ASSIGNDLY expects 14 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's SIGNED generates 8 bits.
```

The part of the statement that says, "but Assign RHS's SIGNED generates 8 bits" is false.  The $signed() method will properly sign extend bit 7 of i_sample_narrow into the additional 6 bits of o_sample_wide when the assignment takes place.

Is this a bug or will verilator perform the sign extension properly?
